title: Bulldog RLR
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=RLR-331 "Bulldog" rocket rifle}}
{{Citation}}
[[File:Star-wars-battlefront-ii-20051019071807597.jpg|thumb|250px|An [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuard]] aiming its Bulldog RLR at a clone trooper.]]
The '''Bulldog Rocket Launching Rifle''' (RLR) was a handheld weapon used by [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|CIS forces]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. This weapon fired powerful antipersonnel rocket propelled grenades.

==Description==
With a chassis akin to the [[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]] used notably by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s, the RLR was primarily an extremely devastating antipersonnel weapon, but was also effective against armored vehicles.

The weapon held a magazine of six rocket-propelled grenades and could fire them rapidly when the weapon was not locked on a target. When locked, the firing rate of the weapon was much slower, but allowed more kill power against small targets. These rockets, with a sluggish fly-time, were most effective when they could catch [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] off-guard or unprepared. The rockets also had a lock-on function, which was easily defeated by ducking behind large obstacles. This aside, the Bulldog RLR was a formidable weapon in the right hands.

==History==
Bulldog RLRs were used most notably by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]]' personal bodyguards—[[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuard]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. By the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Bulldog RLR was employed by many rebels that worked to hinder the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Empire soon acquired this technology and used it in their heavy trooper's weapons, such as the heavy [[Missile launcher/Legends|rocket launcher]]. The Bulldog RLR was almost completely gone after the formation of the Empire, and was seen only in the odd smuggling operation or on the black market. However, [[Gizor Dellso]] used one in the [[Battle of Mustafar (Imperial Period)|Battle of Mustafar]].

==Behind the scenes==
This weapon has a real prototype, the Gyrojet weapon series which includes unificated pistol, carbine and rifle that was created in 1960s in USA. Furthermore, popularization of the image of the reactive small arms these days was significantly contributed by fictional universe Warhammer 40000, where its variant, so-called bolters, is found almost everywhere.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' {{1st}}
